text:
With the rise of the big data era, policymakers today heavily rely on analytics and data derived from massive amounts of data generated by numerous online services, mobile apps, and social media platforms. With machine learning (ML) and artificial intelligence (AI) promising to turn data into the basis for real-time automation of policy-making, there are many technological, legal and social barriers in the public sector that have yet to be addressed. In particular, the AI-based policy-making has to ensure full transparency and explainability of its workings in order to achieve citizen buy-in and ensure compliance with forthcoming regulations such as the EU’s AI Act.

The mentioned complexities and limitations need to be addressed by introducing a technological and operational framework that supports the implementation of trustworthy AI in policy making. In this paper, we introduce: (i) a reference architecture for the application of AI to policy making, (ii) a cloud-based infrastructure known as Virtual Policy Management Environment (VPME) that facilitates the implementation of data-driven policies, (iii) a Machine Learning (ML) approach based on the design of interpretable models through the application of rule-based explanations, and (iv) operational recommendations for ensuring that the implementation of AI-based policy making solutions comply with legislation and risk management practices. Finally, we validate our contributions through four use cases involving municipalities from different countries, where several AI-based policy making solutions were implemented and tested in real world scenarios such as smart parking, maintenance of urban facilities and water resources management. The obtained results show that explainable AI models can be accurate and transparent, which are essential features for the application of AI to policy making and therefore for achieving the objectives of the AI for Policy making initiative.
headline: Fully Human Written
prediction: We believe that this document is fully human-written
